sections.placeholder: Real-time greyscale and colour/spectral Doppler ultrasound of the thyroid. State transducer and approach (e.g. transabdominal/endocavitary).

Nodules — for each: location, size (3 planes, mm), and ACR TI-RADS feature points — composition (cystic/spongiform 0, mixed 1, solid 2) + echogenicity (anechoic 0, hyper/iso 1, hypo 2, very hypo 3) + shape (wider-than-tall 0, taller-than-wide 3) + margin (smooth/ill-defined 0, lobulated/irregular 2, extrathyroidal extension 3) + echogenic foci (none/large comet-tail 0, macrocalcification 1, peripheral/rim 2, punctate echogenic foci 3) = total points → level (TR1 0 / TR2 2 / TR3 3 / TR4 4–6 / TR5 ≥7): 
Cervical lymph nodes (levels, morphology):

sections.label: Impression
sections.placeholder: Concise, prioritised bullets. For each nodule give its summed ACR TI-RADS points, TR level (TR1–TR5) and maximum dimension (mm), leading with the most suspicious. Apply the size thresholds: FNA when TR5 ≥1.0 cm / TR4 ≥1.5 cm / TR3 ≥2.5 cm; follow-up US when TR5 ≥0.5 cm / TR4 ≥1.0 cm / TR3 ≥1.5 cm; otherwise no follow-up. Recommendation from approved follow-up phrasing.
